title: eriastrum pluriflorum subsp. sherman-hoytiae
intro: Eriastrum pluriflorum subsp. sherman-hoytiae is a rare and endemic subspecies of the purple owl's clover, a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Eriastrum pluriflorum subsp. sherman-hoytiae include treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate congestion and soothe the respiratory tract. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized in the treatment of various health conditions.
